context.scenario:
Normally, the rules for my condo building are that I can only use the service lift to take my dog up and down. I can't use the resident's lifts. However, the service lift has been down for months. I live on the 30th floor. So I've been taking the resident's lifts since there's no other option. There's been no issues. My dog is well behaved and has never made a mess. 

Recently one of the condos is undergoing renovation, and so one of the two resident's lifts has been wrapped up on the inside to prevent damage to the lift while renovations are ongoing since they do need a lift to bring stuff up and down. So it's become a make-shift "service lift". 

One of the other residents in the building has taken to screaming at me every time I don't use the wrapped up lift. They say that since I have a dog, I NEED to use the one that's a makeshift service lift. I refuse to. I just don't think it's reasonable since I can't even control which lift gets to me. I just press the button and go in whatever lifts gets to me first. I feel like if she doesn't like dogs for whatever reason, I won't get in a lift if she's already in there, and if I'm already in there, she can just wait for the next one. But that's not good enough apparently. 

I've taken to still using whatever lift reaches me first because I'm not wasting my time like that. She has started to repeatedly complain to me to the building's reception. She's also tried telling me off, but I don't even look at her and ignore her like she doesn't even exist. 

  
AITA for using whatever lift I want to use to take my dog up and down, and also blatantly ignoring a person trying to talk to me?
context.comment:
YTA

  
"There's been no issues." . bullshit. There ARE issues, and they are being reported regularly - you are just ignoring them. Evictions take time to process.



The wrapped up lift is the interim service lift. Use that one, as you agreed to do. 

  
So: You agreed only to use the service lift, and YOU are breaking that deal. "She has started to repeatedly complain to me to the building's reception." - she is handling this well. You are being reported regularly for violating the conditions of your lease.

  
At some point, they will have to react. And you will not like it when that happens.

context.scenario:
My fiancé (M34) and I (F32) are getting married this summer.  
I thought naïvely that this would be a blissful experience of choosing colours and a menu and people being genuinely happy for us and eager to share in our big day.  
As time goes on everyone on his side just keeps adding cherries to the cake of drama.

1. His oldest sister wants to invite an estranged niece, which means my fiancé's brother (and one of the groomsmen) would refuse to come. We don't want the estranged niece there, we have no contact with her. My fiancé and I want his brother from California there.
2. We have asked for our special day to be respected by not including pets or children. (I am a firm believer that the people with the worst behaved children and pets are the loudest about this rule) His oldest sister has spoken against this too and claims that her daughter and SIL won't be able to attend because of their small children (1 & 3) and that this is selfish of us. She's putting enormous pressure on him and my other half is a huge empath and this is eating him up to not be able to please her. AITA for not yielding on this request? All I want is one day of bliss and no infants bawling as I walk down the aisle or while speeches are being made.
3. and this is our grand finale: Other than his brother in California, his other three groomsmen are in Eastern Canada. We have his best man, who is confirmed that he'll be there and convinced he's going to drive because he's scared of airplanes.... and the other two. Both of his other groomsmen are lifelong friends and my fiancé is in touch with them on a weekly basis. Neither have jobs... or savings... or life aspirations. They both thought that we were going to fly them out here... and fit the bill for food, accommodations, their suits too. Don't get me wrong, my fiancé and I are doing okay in the finance department, we budget quite ruthlessly but we don't have and extra 3k kicking around to fly friends out to our wedding. My fiancé is losing sleep because he thinks that no one will be there for him on our special day, and I've apologized saying I'm sorry but it's not something that we can do. We're saving to start a family, quite literally because mother nature's thrown us a curveball and we need to do IVF. We sent the invitations out well in advance, if his two, life-long friends really wanted to be there they would find a way. Currently neither have a job and if they worked part time flipping burgers for a month they would have the cash to make it to their best friends wedding. AITA for being beyond disappointed that they can't get their act together and be there for him?
context.comment:
YTA

"His oldest sister wants to invite " .. this is easy: Not HER wedding, she does not get to invite.

  
"We have asked for our special day to be respected by not including pets or children. " .. why ask? TELL them: No kids, no pets. - Some will not come, but that is something you will have to live with.





As for the groomsmen: You KNEW they were not be able to afford it - either find someone else, or pay for them. They are not doing anything wrong. 



  
"My fiancé is losing sleep because he thinks that no one will be there for him on our special day, and I've apologized saying I'm sorry but it's not something that we can do. " .. s**o you are spending a lot on the things YOU want, but there  "is no money" for what is important for the groom.  - Weddings are the time where the true character is shown. - You COULD do it:** Invite only half of the planned guests, and fly in those that are important to him. **You just DON'T WANT to do it, because you don't like them, and have other priorities you want the money spent on.**



"AITA for being beyond disappointed that they can't get their act together and be there for him?" .. yes, that's ridiculous.

context.scenario:
I'm currently in the last stretch of classes and about to wrap up my first year at college. My roommate...if you were to ask me a couple of months ago how I felt about her. I would've said she was great. But, something clicked in my head recently and I realized she's been benefiting a lot from me.

I never felt valued by her as much as she says she feels valued by me.

My roommate lost her dorm key last semester and I agreed to work with her by leaving our dorm door open so she could come in any time until she got her new key. This arrangement stayed in place for months until I realized a lot of things about our relationship.

For some context: recently, she's been staying at her friend's dorm across the street. she also lives near by, and she tends to go home a lot, so throughout the year, she never was in our dorm much.

We had a brief fight over text and when I saw that nothing was getting resolved, I put the nail down and said, here's what's going to happen moving forward:

'hey! trust, you're doing great and i'm not making this a big deal, but would it be possible for you to avoid coming between the hours of 12am and 6am? i bring this up because i would like to keep the door locked. even if you were to come back later. i know you mention that you won't come too late sometimes, but i usually would like to be asleep by 12am. and i also wouldn't like to be woken up unless its morning and i would have to get up anyway. of course you know that because you get the RA when you need to and you mention it to me before that you wouldn't like to wake me up and i appreciate that a lot. and i appreciate that you do adjust, but i was hoping to have this more set in stone and clear.’

overall, it's a safety thing. some people in our dorm building are strange and someone tried to come in last semester at 3am. but yeah, unless you're certain you would come back within 15 - 30 mins-like exactly 100% positively certain (i would then keep it unlocked but make that be during the daylight hours) i would like to keep it locked!'

Agreement didn’t last long. Around 2:30am, she came by and start knocking. She wouldn't stop and it pissed me off, so l opened it. The next time, I planned to leave her there.

So, she came knocking and she knocked and spammed called me for 3 minutes. I opened the door.

I texted her tonight and asked her to not come at those hours anymore, and she asked me where she would sleep. And, after her other friend wanted space, she started to sleep here a bit more frequently.

Note: my roommate reached out to our housing director for a new key shortly atter I set the dnd 12am-6am rule, but the director says it's too late and we're moving out soon.

I also have 8:30am's every morning. i wouldn't like to have my sleep disturbed.

That's what's going on for me now and I feel like shit whenever I have to constantly address my boundaries. And, I’m absolutely terrified to push back on her. 

AITA for locking her out at night?
context.comment:
YTA

  
"Agreement didn’t last long. Around 2:30am, she came by and start knocking. She wouldn't stop and it pissed me off, so l opened it. The next time, I planned to leave her there." .. **and she will knock until you open.** If that does not work, she probably will have to call a locksmith or emergency services to have her door opened. - And sue you for the cost caused by you locking her out of her room. And: If she is petty, she will not have the door repaired until move out date.

  
This is her room, you can not lock her out.

context.scenario:
There's gonna be a few paragraphs of background to preface the actual event. I highly recommend reading it because I definitely sound like an asshole without context and I want a real and true reading of my situation.

For context, I (16f, not sure if this is relevant or not) have a lot of online friends. I was basically given unrestricted internet access since I was a kid, so I handle online friendships better than my IRL friends do. I'm a lot more attached to my online friends than most people would be, which my family friends don't like. More context, our family friend group is shallow but had a lot of history. I've always been chronically online, so when I got a phone, I strained my relationship with the group because I was always on it, so I understand why they dislike it when I'm on it. I've been disrespected by a lot of our friends and manhandled (slammed into a wall, crawled on top of, etc) b/c my phone. It's gotten to a point where I can't be on my phone for more than five minutes at a time at hours-long events without being dogged on.

Onto the actual thing, sorry if you were expecting something big. Small context, a portion of our friend group meets up for UNO a few times a month, and I had a new online friend group form a few months ago.

At these hangouts, I usually go on my phone. Nobody really cared too much because all of the people there literally just did not care to include me (the adults made me feel more included when playing UNO and I HATE hanging with the adults), so I did what I wanted. During the event, I was on a call but you couldn't really tell because I usually text when I can't talk.

Like I said, anti-climactic situation. There wasn't a fight, we just had an awkward conversation after they literally grabbed my phone out of my hands while playing mafia because I had my eyes closed. I ended up telling them I was on a call, they made a few snarky comments before one of them had to leave and the hangout devolved until they were watching America's Got Talent auditions.

I think it's pretty obvious that they think I'm annoying and a jerk for not hanging out with them and calling people outside the friend group instead of talking to them, and I understand how it looks because despite playing mafia with them, I was pretty checked out of the game and I was talking to my other friends instead. I get that it's a jerk move to be checked out of a social event, but it's been four years and I really cannot capture how absolutely terrible it was to be around them when they acted like every time I pulled my phone out I was going to fall back into my 13-year-old self, and then have them completely ignore me in favor of each other and THEN getting mad when I got tired of it and went back on my phone to talk to people who actually gave a shit about me. The only reason I'm putting this here is because I used to go to a therapist to reduce my phone activity so I might be looking at this situation in a skewed manner. So am I the asshole?
context.comment:
NTA

  
"they literally grabbed my phone out of my hands" --- THis makes THEM the AH.

  
"which my family friends " ... sounds like they re not your friends, they are your parent's friend's kids. No need to like them, find your own frineds.

  
"At these hangouts, I usually go on my phone." .. stop going to these hangouts.



  
" I've been disrespected by a lot of our friends and manhandled (slammed into a wall, crawled on top of, etc)" .. tell a mandated reporter at school about the abuse, and go no contact with these AHs. find better friends.

context.scenario:
So here’s the deal. I drive to work every day, and a while back my coworker’s car broke down. I gave him a ride once because, you know, I felt bad and it was on my way anyway. No big deal.

But then it just… never stopped. Like, now he basically expects me to pick him up every single day. He doesn’t offer gas money, he’s made me late a couple of times because he wasn’t ready, and honestly it’s starting to piss me off.

I finally told him I’m not his personal Uber and he needs to figure something out. He got super mad and apparently told other people at work that I’m selfish. A couple of coworkers even said I should just suck it up since it “doesn’t cost a lot.” And also he goes he helped me a lot but I don’t remember I got help from him a lot to be honest. I only asked him if he had extra pen to borrow once. That’s all! he was lying too.

I don’t think it’s fair though. I was trying to help one time, not sign up to be his chauffeur forever. But now I’m wondering if I was too harsh.

AITA for cutting him off?
context.comment:
NTA

stop driving him. And: 1$ per mile, cash up front. It's his share on wear and tear, gas, and your effort.  - don't make it cheaper than owing his oen car on him.



LEt the other coworkers drive him then, if it is not a big thing.



And: If yo drivr him, make a rule: On time, at your house, or you leave without him.



  
But: If you want to avoid the hassle, make it unreliable fo him: "Tomorrow you have to find something else, I am visiting mom before work." - Today you need to make your way hhome alone, I am visiting a friend directly after work".  - "or simply "sorry, today I am picking up stuff before work, can't wait.". - make sure to leavve him hanging, and make it "sorry, I would love to help, but tomorrow sdimply does not work for me" - or "sorry, you were late, and In eeded to be at the office on time".

context.scenario:
I (23f) recently started a new job as server at a restaurant this week. It's been okay, I honestly don't like how everyone is overly friendly at work but a job is a job right?

This afternoon, I finally meet this co-worker (32m) who works from Wednesdays to Sundays. I shook his hands when he came in to introduce myself. He raised my hand up, looked at me up and down, and spun me around 360 to check out my body.

I was just in shock, I didn't know what was happening. He then told me I had a nice figure and he wishes he had a butt like mine.

At that point, I was all red. I was just like what?? and he immediately apologized, saying he was just complimenting my body and then asked for my gym routine. 

Other co-workers were around us and they saw I was clearly uncomfortable so they jumped in and just told me he is just like that. In the background, I heard him trying to tell me he is gay and he didn't mean anything bad by the comment.

But at that point, my head was spinning and I was infuriated. Sure I had men catcalled me and said inappropriate things but not like this, not in workplace by a co-worker, not with other co-workers watching. It was a really embarrassing situation.

I clocked out about half an hour after that. I worked day shift and he usually does night shift, so luckily we didn't have to work together today. He just got here a little early and spent the rest of the time in the break room talking with others. He seems super loud and way too friendly with others as well, he doesn't watch what he says.

I immediately texted my manager, who didn't come in because it was his only day off. I then emailed the HR department including all details and asked for my co-worker to be fired because what he did and said was simply too inappropriate. He seems to be a popular guy at work and brings in a lot of tips so I don't know if they will really fire him or just give a warning.

AITA for asking him to be fired? Am I going too far?
context.comment:
YTA

  
Reporting it was fine, but asking for him to be fired is overstepping. That'S not your decission - you just told them: YOu are unwilling to even try to work in a workplace where he is - And xou told them one of you will have to be removed. 

  
So: The reasonable expectation is: He will get a warning, and you will be let go at some point.



And: " He raised my hand up, looked at me up and down, and spun me around 360 to check out my body." .. this is not something that can happen without your willing participation. Most of us won't just spin around for a coworker.

context.scenario:
My husband likes his beer. He has different shifts so his weekends are not the same as mine. He has periods he drinks more then I would like. Once we had a discussion and he agreed he drank too much. We discussed together he would not drink more than 4 bottles of beer if he had to take care of the kids the next day because I have to work. Last weeks he says he cant remember that we agreed to this. Tonight I allready asked him at dinner if he please could not drink more than the agreed 4 bottles. I had 2 beers, we were enjoying a show together. He got up and got a 5th beer. I got angry and asked him if he was being serious. He does not get the point he says. He thinks I am overreacting and whining and that I ruïned his night. He went to sleep in the attick. He does not want to sleep in the same bed with me tonight, he is fed up with me for now.

So am I the asshole for telling him he should not drink that much?
context.comment:
NTA

  
But: He is an alcoholic - 4 beers every day? That's 

  
The WHO (World health organization) considers alcohol consumption on more thhan three days a week to be problematic behavior - your partner is a habitual drinker, and he can't even consrain him to "only" 4 beers.

  
Break up now, this will only get worse.

context.scenario:
So to start this off we go back 2 weeks to a question i was asked by one of my best friends, he asked me what my finances are. When i answered him, he waits until we are face to face to ask me if id be ok giving up my saturday so he could have an extra day, I tell him I cant without going behind on my bills but maybe around july when my bills are straight i should be able to

Now about 2 months ago he asked if id take his shift so he could spend more time with his gf. My shift at The time was 4 days a week and his was 5 days. I agreed because i needed the money for my bills as im barely scraping by.
He is now asking for the saturday back due to they found out last week they are expecting and the guy who they are renting with rn is supposedly "throwing them out" come july

 I explained to him that I couldnt and that me and my gf wont have a place to stay at the end of June due to her getting kicked out of her sisters home and my family not allowing her and her cat to live with me

He then calculated mine and my gf's finances from 2 weeks ago when he asked me about them and told me i could afford to give him the day.

I got severly offended by this and told him no i cant give him the day and told him if he needs money that bad to stop buying frivilous things that im aware they do spend on or to get a second job like he has had in the past or that it doesnt help too much that his gf is part time and only works 4 days a week that are 5 hours long.

I explained that i dont even have the money right now to buy normal groceries from my bills i owe on, which are from financial decisions before i lost my last job or unexpected expense. Not me being wreckless.

He then tells me he offered only to take the Saturday and that if he wants he could use his seniority to make use swap shifts entirely (he knows i prefer nights cause my gf works nights)
He also brings up how he got me my last job and the one we are currently working at and that im being ungrateful to him.

I explain to him that at the past job and this one ive done nothing but cover days for him despite me never taking one off myself and that at this one he asked me to cover a day before i started along with working a double into a quick turnaround when i didnt even have training yet.

I found out through the guy he rents from that he already asked our boss for the day before asking if i was ok with it and that he isnt getting kicked out in july and that its a slight possibility but probably not. Which means this is their choice not out of nessecity. And they arent going for something cheap


And i want to clarify how I always bend over backwards for him and his gf doing this like working on their thing lr other things
And i wish i was painting him in a bad light but i seriously cant think of a time where he seriously helped me out.

I explained that seniority at the job or not on a friend level it makes me feel like an asset.

So AITA for saying no to my friend and being upset with him over this?
context.comment:
NTa

  
And: Don't believe him. If he forces a shift change, he will have to work nightrs. Which he does not want, or he would already be doing it, due to his senority.



Talk to your boss, and tell him you don't agree with the swap. 

  
AND: Stop talkong to that quy except strictly business. He is NOT your friend.

context.scenario:
Today I(17f) went to work as usual and my boss(40s? f)left me her regular vague list of things to do along with verbal instructions. In these instructions she specifically told me to take a tray of candles off the rack because they were “done”. She left me to go to her office like normal and I was working on a separate task when my coworker came in to help me. I asked her to take the trays down since they were done and she began trimming the wicks. It wasn’t until she made it through about 2 trays that she realized the candles were in fact not done, they still needed a final pour. She told me she would talk to my boss since I’ve always been a bit intimidated by her. She left without speaking to her so I had to explain what happened. I told my boss that I had her remove the trays and never saw them myself, assuming they were done since that’s what I was told. I also told her it was my mistake for not checking the trays. My boss proceeds to go on to say that she never told me they were done and that I just cost her an extreme amount of money and I had 0 authorization to tell someone what to do. I tried to explain to her that there must have been some miscommunication and I believed the candles were done and I hadn’t looked at them myself since I was working on something else. She proceeds to repeatedly say that she has no clue why I would think that and why I let it happen and that I wasted so much money and time fully blaming it on me. I apologized more times than I can count during this time. She then told me to go home and proceeded to fire me through text about and hour later. 
I just want to know if Im in the wrong or what. I was spoken to in a way that made me sound idiotic and horrible. I took accountability and profusely apologized to her and still was spoken to in what I and my family feels to be an inappropriate manner. 
EDIT: there have been a few incidents in the past of her giving me instructions then turning around later claiming I did something wrong and she did not tell me to do something even if I had done it the exact way I was initially told
context.comment:
" also told her it was my mistake for not checking the trays. " .. it weas NOt.

  
You did not tell the other employe to trim the wicks, If she had just teken them down as you told her, tehre would have been no damage.



NTA

  
Find another job.

context.scenario:
A couple days ago I left work an hour and a half late. Due to the nature of my work, I am not able to have my cellphone on me and I am not able to leave without someone replacing me. My replacement was late due to a personal emergency. So I was not able to let him know I’d be late. Once I was able to use my phone, I let him know what happened, apologized, and told him I’d be home soon. 

When I was finally home, he was being short with me for the rest of the night. After the kids went to bed, I told him he needs to talk. I knew it was about me getting home late, and I apologized again and told him I would have warned him if I was able to. 

He told me I should have left at my scheduled time no matter what. He had plans with people and had to miss them because I was late and he was home with the kids. He said it’s also not the first time, which is true, it’s happened a few times over the last couple years, but never this long. He said in the future, I need to leave on time or text him and ask if I am able to stay late. 

AITA for leaving work and not warning my husband?
context.comment:
YTA

  
"He had plans with people and had to miss them because I was late and he was home with the kids. He said it’s also not the first time, " .. so you are unreliable, and you can not be trusted to keep your commitments. that makes you a shitty partner.

  
"With my job, if I am not there, work stops" ... This happens all the time. YOu have a walkie talkie. --- you can not make a phone call. But you COULD prepare, and have someone else send your husband a text. You just don'T care enough to treart him like a partner.

context.scenario:
AITA... for wanting these people to sign a legal & notarized contract with us about this vehicle...


let me set the mood for you, husband (40) went on Valentine's Day Eve to (co-sign) for a vehicle for his Male co -worker (42) of only 60 days.


 I expressed to him that if he decided to do so it would completely screw us out of our pre approval for our house loan that would be coming up in merely 20 days that we've been working on building his credit score up to get the rate and terms that we have been looking for. His ex wife from 12 years ago completely botched his credit with many small loans and it has taken SO LONG to build it back up. 
I emphasized that it would really hurt our process & to take that into consideration..
He comes home early from work 02/13/25 & says he's got to go do some shopping for Valentine's Day stuff because he forgot a few things...I have a bad feeling about it so I check his location & see he's actually turning into our local Dodge dealership. I nonchalantly text him and tell him I have a bad feeling about this whole ordeal and he said "everything will be fine"
I again say I hope you don't do this because it'll really screw us. 
He then admits that he's at the dealership with the guy and is about to sign papers & that I'm stressing him out.. I tell him hold on I'm coming up there to look over the papers before he signs. I get there within 7 mins of that first text that I'm coming & text to tell him "I'm here what office are you in" & he texts back "too late" 😡 
I immediately run through the dealership doors and co-worker is not in the office with my husband apparently they are already in mystery vehicle getting ready to drive off. I ask him how much did he co-sign for and he just says (idk) I ask him where his copies of his paper work are that he signed and "he doesn't have any"

 Finance manager hasn't printed anything off yet. I ask finance manager how much did he just sign for. He says 44k but with interest it's 73 THOUSAND US DOLLARS in total!!!!!!!!!!!! 
Y'all I about CROAKED!! 
Get the paper work and there's a woman's name on it!!!!! & not only did he sign these papers he actually CO-BOUGHT with the co-workers WIFE because Co-Worker doesn't have a DL turns out 😵‍💫😵‍💫😵‍💫😵‍💫😵‍💫 I AM FUMING!😤 & he says I'm just making a drama of it. Don't I have every right to be completely and utterly pissed the hell off?? Also it's a Pearl white Cadillac Escalade premium luxury 4x4 to add insult to injury 🤣 

Accidentally left out a very crucial detail... this A1 co-worker that my husband needed so badly to run his rig operation QUIT 28 days into this whole car deal.... so now they don't even work on together. Really seems like they just used him for his high credit score & somewhat of a good heart.

If someone can tell me how to add pictures to these posts I'll show screen shots lol
context.comment:
NTA

  
"His ex wife from 12 years ago completely botched his credit with many small loans and it has taken SO LONG to build it back up. " .. don't blame his ex wife. THis is the same thing his next partner will say about you



  
So: Your partner is financially irresponsible, Divorce him and get out while you still can.

context.scenario:
My son (21) is currently in the process of moving out. His room was always rather messy and he kept a lot of things he didn't need. He got the first few boxes to his apartment a few days ago with his essentials, mainly electronics and some clothes. 

In the meantime I wanted to help out by sorting through some of the stuff he doesn't need. He still has most of his binders from highschool, books he hasn't touched in years, stuff like that, which I sorted out. He also has a few stuffed animals. He's always been a bit childish, though most of them were either in his closet or on top of a shelf. Those went in the bin, because I figured he doesn't need them. Same with the gigantic shark that was on his bed. That one turned out to be an issue to throw away. I don't even remember when he got it, but apparently it has value. 

Now he's mad at me and has been ignoring me. While I do think he's acting a bit ridiculous about a stuffed animal, being treated like this upsets me. I do feel bad, but I'm not sure if I actually did anything wrong.
context.comment:
YTA

  
You got rid of his stuffed animal, he got rid of you. Sounds like a reasonable reaction. 

  
Go through the trash to find it - or to the dumpsite - if you find it and clean it, he might be unwise enough to allow you back into his life.

scenario: Let me just start by saying I haven't done it yet but I am thinking about doing this. (Hence why I'm on here). I 26F am only allergic to 2 things, perfumed lotions and AXE body spray. I work with a bunch of males. My assistant director and all my supervisors know what I am allergic to. They have told everyone what I am allergic to. But there is this one asshole that I work with that doesn't want to listen or just hates me. The other day I was working a full 12 hour shift that included a lot of manual labor. I looked at who I was going to be working with that day and he was scheduled to be working with me. I already knew that it was going to be a long day but that made it worse. Him and I got to work at the same time and I was all ready to go. He got some of his stuff ready and pulled out of his locker some are body spray and I asked him not to spray that on today. He looked me dead in my face and did it anyway. Within an hour I broke out in hives. I showed it to my boss and he just told me to take 2 benadryl and see how I feel in 2 hours. I was not happy with that response but I took the medication anyways and drew circles on my skin to see if it would still spread and wiped down the areas with alcohol wipes to prevent it from spreading. Well let's just say it spread even more because I was with him all day. Well needless to say I got sent home early because of how I was feeling and he got off scott free. That really pisses me off. So I was thinking, wibtah if I changed his body spray with perfume instead?
question: Based on this person's historical commenting patterns, which moral principle would they MOST likely use to judge this scenario?
answer_options: A. Property/Consent: The judgment is primarily justified by violations involving ownership, money, physical possessions, or bodily access without permission.
B. Fairness/Reciprocity: The judgment is primarily justified by proportionality, equity, or reciprocal treatment (fair share, equal standards, tit-for-tat).
C. Autonomy/Boundaries: The judgment is primarily justified by violation of personal sovereignty: controlling behavior, unwanted interference, privacy invasion, or boundary crossing (non-material).
D. Role-based Responsibility: The judgment is primarily justified by duties tied to a specific functional role (tenant/roommate/employee/co-parent) rather than emotion-based loyalty.
